steps.title: Combine constants
steps.content: Drop the parentheses and add: $(p+3)+8=p+11$. The equation becomes $$p+11=10.$$

steps.title: Solve for p
steps.content: Subtract $11$ from both sides: $p=10-11=-1$. That matches choice A — A is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice A is correct. Subtracting $8$ from both sides of the given equation yields $p+3=2$. Subtracting $3$ from both sides of this equation yields $p=-1$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
